title: Senior Manager, Legal & Claims with a global shipping MNC based in town
description:
• Requires 10+ years of post-qualification experience (PQE) in maritime law, gained either at a shipping firm or in-house at a shipping company.
  • Shipping litigation experience with experience dealing with ship owners is essential
  • Has deep understanding of admiralty law, and international maritime conventions.

Our client is a global shipping MNC (based in town). The co allows for a WFH arrangement of 2 times a week (after the candidate passes the probation period). This is a perm role at the group level (based in Singapore). The role reports directly to the Group General Counsel.

Summary: To provide all legal support to the Company covering mainly corporate, compliance, contracts and insurance functions.

Reports to: Group General Counsel

Location: Singapore

Role and Responsibilities:

LEGAL MATTERS

  • Prepare agency agreement for all the Group’s shipping agents.
  • Draft commercial agreements, including but not limited to Connecting Carrier Agreements (CCAs), Slot Exchange Agreements, Joint Service Agreements, Joint Venture Agreements, Slot Charter Agreements and other agreements and contracts.
  • Vet and recommend changes to draft agreements and contracts received from other parties within the Group.
  • Reviewing terminal agreements.
  • Provide legal advice and recommend solutions to disputes that may arise regarding business agreements and contracts.
  • Liaise with external lawyers on all legal matters.
  • Review and negotiate MOA on sale and purchase of ships; and assist closing.

CHARTER PARTY MATTERS

  • Negotiate charter party clauses and finalize the charter with owners.
  • Handles charter party disputes by liaising with P&I club.
  • Draft internal charterparties and negotiate with external charterparties for owned ships.

INSURANCE MATTERS

  • Assist fleet department and business hubs on all queries (coverage, claims and certificates, etc) on insurance by liaising with brokers.
  • Liaise closely with insurance brokers on development in P&I and changes in the marine insurance industry.

HANDLING CLAIMS

  • Handle all cargo/container claims and monitor progress; Liaise with P&I clubs’ claim handler on claims progress.
  • Liaise with Line Managers / Operations on appointment of surveyor to determine the cause of the incident and the extent of the damage/loss.
  • Take steps on recovery against responsible party liable for damage / loss arising from claim incident.
  • Consider loss prevention measures with Operations and implementation.
  • Liaise with Fleet Management on claims arising from internal vessels.
  • Provide assistance / advice to claims arising in other hubs.
  • Defend claims and deny liability against claimants.

OTHERS

  • Contract management, operational advisory and compliance matters.

Requirements:

  • Education: A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Law (LLB/LLM) from a recognized university.
  • Experience: Requires 10+ years of post-qualification experience (PQE) in maritime law, gained either at a shipping firm or in-house at a shipping company. Shipping litigation experience with experience dealing with ship owners is a must.
  • Hard Skills: Deep understanding of admiralty law, and international maritime conventions.
  • Certification: Qualification to practice as a solicitor or barrister in a common law jurisdiction (e.g., UK or Singapore) is preferred.

Selling pts:

  • Global shipping MNC based in town. This role is a global role at the HQ level based in Singapore
  • Role is based in town. A WFH arrangement of 2 days per week is allowed (after probation clearance)

To apply, pl send your cv in word doc to resume@cap-cg.com. Pl also include details on your current salary, expected salary and notice period in your cv.

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Posted by:

CAP Consulting (EA license: 14C7175)

Caroline Poh (EA Registration: R1105649)

Date ad is posted - 31 January 2026

postedCompany.description:
CAP Consulting is a regional boutique executive recruitment firm with a focus on Singapore and the Asia region. The firm was established in 2014 by Caroline Poh, a veteran finance search professional with more than 20 years of search and recruitment experience. Its focus is to provide a results oriented and solutions based approach to executive recruitment and search that is vital in the increasingly dynamic and complex marketplace.  


In the financial service sector, we cover roles across sectors including front line/investment, middle office, finance and operations. Specific roles that we work on include General Management, Investment, M&A, Sales, Strategy Risk, Compliance, Consulting, Treasury, Corporate Finance, Operations, IA and other corporate functions ( Corporate Affairs, HR, Accounting).  


For the sectors outside of financial service, we also cover Financial and Professional Services, Commodity, Consumer, Healthcare/ Pharmaceutical, Industrial, Logistics, Manufacturing, Oil & Gas, Technology and Telecommunications. The roles that we specialise in include Finance/Accounting, HR, General Management, Technology, Sales & Marketing, Legal/Compliance/Risk, Business Development/Commercial, Operations etc.
postedCompany.name: THE CAP CONSULTING GROUP PTE. LTD.
